subject: Rebase PR #17 (agent-1/services) onto updated main and verify build
description:
REQUEST_ID: merge-all-open-prs
DOMAIN: services
ASSIGNED_TO: worker-1
FILES: src/services/dataFeed.js, src/services/kalshiApi.js, src/services/kalshiWebSocket.js, src/services/mockData.js, src/hooks/useKalshiData.js
VALIDATION: tier2
TIER: 3
PRIORITY: URGENT

## Task: Rebase agent-1 branch (PR #17) onto main

Main has moved forward since your branch was created. You need to rebase agent-1 onto the current main and resolve any conflicts.

## Steps

```bash
# 1. Navigate to your worktree and checkout the branch
cd /path/to/your/worktree  # you're already here
git fetch origin
git checkout agent-1 2>/dev/null || git checkout -b agent-1 origin/agent-1
git pull origin agent-1

# 2. Rebase onto main
git rebase origin/main
```

## If Conflicts Arise

```bash
# See which files conflict
git status

# For each conflicting file:
# - src/services/*.js or src/hooks/useKalshiData.js: KEEP YOUR (agent-1) version
#   git checkout --theirs <file>  (theirs = your branch = agent-1 commits being rebased)
# - src/components/scanners/ files: KEEP MAIN's version (already updated)
#   git checkout --ours <file>  (ours = main during rebase)
# - dist/ files: DISCARD (remove them)
#   git rm -f dist/assets/* dist/index.html 2>/dev/null || true
# - .claude/ files: KEEP MAIN's version
#   git checkout --ours .claude/

git add -A
git rebase --continue
```

**IMPORTANT about --ours/--theirs during rebase:**
During `git rebase`, the naming is REVERSED:
- `--ours` = main (the base you're rebasing onto)
- `--theirs` = your commits being replayed

## After Rebase

```bash
# Force push the rebased branch
git push --force-with-lease origin agent-1

# Build verification
npm install && npm run build
```

If build fails, fix the issue before marking complete.

## Completion

1. Update worker-status.json: set your status to 'completed_task', current_task = null
2. Append to change-summaries.md: note that agent-1 branch rebased and PR #17 ready to merge
3. Touch .claude/signals/.completion-signal
